seoTitle: Harris Park vs Hartley Vale — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Harris Park and Hartley Vale. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Harris Park vs Hartley Vale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Harris Park and Hartley Vale.
heading: Harris Park vs Hartley Vale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Harris Park, New South Wales and Hartley Vale, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hartley Vale has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Harris Park (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Harris Park or Hartley Vale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Harris Park has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Hartley Vale scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Harris Park scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Hartley Vale scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Harris Park or Hartley Vale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hartley Vale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
